leaderRationale: Pushback is required because both reviewers returned reject with unresolved major findings, and the self-check independently confirms the canonical-output mismatch. The issues are material but fixable role-package design/governance defects rather than a non-recoverable execution failure, so blocked is not warranted.
unresolved: Canonical artifact declarations do not name the exact required `tools/agent-role-builder/role/...` paths, and `<steps>`, `<outputs>`, and `<completion>` do not define one authoritative per-terminal-state artifact lifecycle.

Governance semantics diverge from the approved review model: freeze is defined as unanimous approval instead of no unresolved blocking/major issues with `approved`/`conditional`, `changes_required` is invented, arbitration is incompletely specified, and Step 3 overclaims current self-check behavior.

Authority and durable identity are not source-aligned: static docs are treated as operative superiors instead of reference evidence/live governance, and the contract intent is bootstrap/run-specific rather than the role's persistent purpose.
ARB-006, ARB-007, ARB-018, ARB-021, and ARB-022 remain unresolved without rule text or semantic closure, so compliance cannot be certified.
improvementsApplied: All required top-level XML tags are present, and Step 3 enumerates the exact required tag set.
The workflow already separates leader drafting, self-check, board review, and terminal resolution.
The draft distinguishes canonical, evidence, and internal run artifacts and already carries provenance/baseline/resume concepts.

conceptual_groups.summary: Artifact lifecycle is not fully specified against the contract, so canonical outputs and completion cannot be verified mechanically.

findings.description: The <outputs> section uses basename/template references like <slug>-role.md instead of the exact canonical required_outputs paths, so the declared canonical artifacts in the contract are not actually named in the role package.
The self-check already reports missing required outputs for all four canonical files, confirming that the draft's output declarations do not align with the contract's required output paths.

The <completion> section says 'all applicable artifacts written to output directory' without defining the artifact set for frozen vs pushback vs blocked vs resume_required, leaving terminal-state completion ambiguous.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Define one authoritative artifact matrix. List every canonical artifact with its full tool-relative path under tools/agent-role-builder/role/, list every run-scoped artifact under tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<job-id>/, and state exactly which artifacts must exist for each terminal state.

conceptual_groups.summary: Authority and governance semantics mix source-backed rules with local inventions, which makes the role package overclaim behavior and authority.

findings.description: The authority chain treats docs/v0/architecture.md and docs/VISION.md as items the role is 'subordinate to', which conflates reference evidence with operative authority and leaves precedence unclear.

The draft says freeze happens only when every reviewer approves, but the approved review-process architecture allows freeze when reviewers approve or go conditional after minor fixes. This is a changed governance rule, not a restatement.
The draft introduces 'changes_required' as a blocking verdict even though the board review contract uses approved | conditional | reject. That is an invented semantic.

Step 4 says 'Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits' without carrying the approved minor-only limitation, actor/evidence model, or result semantics from the review-process architecture. This is an incomplete local arbitration model.

Step 3 claims self-check covers structural output validation and lifecycle consistency, but the current validator only checks tag presence, role-name presence, out-of-scope keyword coverage, required output mentions, and result reference. The role package overstates implemented behavior.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Recast authority as an explicit precedence chain with live governance first and static docs as reference evidence only. Restate freeze, verdict, arbitration, and self-check semantics exactly as approved in the governing architecture or label them explicitly as target-state rather than current behavior.

conceptual_groups.summary: Scope and identity text mixes exclusions with clarifications and embeds run-specific context in durable fields.

findings.description: The Not in scope list duplicates 'Application workflow creation' and embeds positive in-scope clarifications inside exclusion bullets, which blurs scope polarity.
The contract summary intent is run-specific ('Resume and finalize...') rather than describing the enduring purpose of the role package.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Keep out-of-scope items as pure exclusions, deduplicated once each. Move bootstrap/resume context into business_context or run evidence, and keep canonical intent timeless.

residual_risks: If this draft is frozen as-is, future reviewers and callers will not have a single authoritative artifact-path matrix for promotion vs run evidence.
The role package may govern the board with semantics that differ from the approved review-process architecture, creating drift between documentation, prompts, and execution.
Because Step 3 overclaims self-check behavior, later operators may assume lifecycle/structural guarantees that the validator does not actually enforce.
strengths: All required top-level XML tags are present, so tag completeness is structurally close.
The core mission is clear: governed role-package creation with multi-LLM review and pushback instead of guessing.
The draft correctly keeps tool creation and code execution out of scope and preserves baseline/resume concepts for update and fix flows.

conceptual_groups.summary: Canonical artifact outputs lack directory paths, making artifact lifecycle unresolvable by tooling

findings.description: The <outputs> section lists canonical artifacts as '<slug>-role.md', '<slug>-role-contract.json', '<slug>-decision-log.md', '<slug>-board-summary.md' without specifying the target directory. The self-check correctly flags all four as MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT because the 'tools/agent-role-builder/role/' prefix is absent. Downstream tooling and reviewers cannot determine where to write or verify these files.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Add explicit directory paths to every canonical artifact entry in <outputs>. Either use full paths ('tools/agent-role-builder/role/<slug>-role.md') or declare a 'canonical_directory: tools/agent-role-builder/role/' header at the top of the canonical block. The self-check must pass with zero MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT findings before the package qualifies for board review.

conceptual_groups.summary: Contract intent field describes a transient run operation, not the role's durable purpose

findings.description: The contract 'intent' reads: 'Resume and finalize the agent-role-builder self-role using improved review process (structured feedback, split-verdict, arbitration).' This is a one-time bootstrap directive, not the role's stable identity. A role contract's intent must describe what the role permanently does so it remains valid across all future invocations, not just the current run.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Rewrite 'intent' to describe the role's persistent purpose, e.g., 'Create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM board review, producing frozen packages only when no material pushback remains.' Move any run-specific resumption context (prior run state, process improvements being tested) to the request JSON, not the role contract.

conceptual_groups.summary: Five ARB rules non-compliant at heuristic level, passed into board review unresolved

findings.description: ARB-006, ARB-007, ARB-018, ARB-021, ARB-022 all show keyword coverage at or below 45% and are marked 'needs LLM review'. These were not resolved before this review round. The compliance map is forwarded with five open LLM-review flags and no evidence the draft was revised to address them. Without the rule text, the reviewer cannot certify compliance, but the pattern of five co-failing rules at the same low threshold suggests a shared coverage gap rather than isolated noise.
findings.source_section: Compliance map / ARB-006, ARB-007, ARB-018, ARB-021, ARB-022

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Include the full text of each flagged rule in the next review package. Revise the draft to explicitly address ARB-006, ARB-007, ARB-018, ARB-021, and ARB-022. Do not forward unresolved LLM-review flags into the board review round — the self-check step (Step 3) should block promotion when compliance_map contains non_compliant entries marked 'needs LLM review'.

conceptual_groups.summary: Scope section has duplicate entry and embeds an in-scope clarification inside the not-in-scope list

findings.description: 'Application workflow creation' appears twice in the 'Not in scope' list, creating a redundant and potentially confusing entry.

'Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)' places an in-scope affirmation as a parenthetical inside the not-in-scope list. The entire line reads as out-of-scope to a quick scan and requires parsing the parenthetical to extract the actual intent.

conceptual_groups.redesign_guidance: Remove the duplicate 'Application workflow creation' entry. Move the board-review orchestration carve-out to the 'Use when' list as a positive assertion, or add a 'Scope boundary notes' block below the not-in-scope list. Never embed in-scope affirmations inside a not-in-scope enumeration.

residual_risks: If ARB-006, ARB-007, ARB-018, ARB-021, or ARB-022 cover authority escalation or scope-creep prevention, the non-compliance could be blocking under governance rules, not just major.

The bootstrap self-role scenario (agent-role-builder reviewing its own role) is listed in scope but no conflict-of-interest guardrail or special handling is defined — this creates a governance blind spot when the tool evaluates itself.

The contract runtime section specifies watchdog_timeout_seconds=600 and max_launch_attempts=2, but the role's <guardrails> and <completion> are silent on what happens when the watchdog fires mid-round (no partial-freeze, resume-required, or blocked outcome is mapped to a watchdog event).
strengths: All nine required XML tags are present and non-empty.
Authority owns/does-not-own split is cleanly drawn with no ambiguous overlap against llm-tool-builder.

Freeze conditions are precisely specified in both <guardrails> and Step 4: every reviewer must approve and no material issues may remain — the split-verdict arbitration path closes a common governance gap.
Artifact lifecycle table in <outputs> correctly distinguishes canonical, evidence, and internal run artifacts with conditional/always annotations.
Provenance attachment is explicit in <guardrails> and applies to all operations and artifacts.
Step 3 self-check validates the required XML tag set explicitly, providing a machine-verifiable completeness gate before board review.
context-gathering section correctly differentiates preconditions (Steps 0-before-1) from sequential loading steps, which prevents undefined-state errors on resume.
